issues.description: The lesson mentions HTTP status codes but could include a quick reference table for the most common codes students will use in the capstone
issues.webResearchNote: A visual reference card showing 200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 500 with brief descriptions would be helpful for quick reference during implementation.

issues.description: The lesson covers SecurityFilterChain configuration well but could explicitly show a complete example of the SecurityConfig class with JWT filter integration
issues.webResearchNote: Students need to see the complete wiring: SecurityFilterChain bean, PasswordEncoder bean, and JwtAuthenticationFilter integration in one place.

issues.description: The lesson for React path students should include a note about CORS configuration being required on the backend (covered in M13.6) since this is easily forgotten
issues.webResearchNote: Cross-reference to M13.6 CORS content would help students remember to configure this before trying to connect frontend to backend.

issues.description: The lesson covers integration but could include a troubleshooting checklist for common connection issues (CORS errors, port mismatches, authentication failures)
issues.webResearchNote: Common issues: 1) Spring Boot not running on expected port, 2) CORS not configured, 3) React dev server proxy not set up, 4) JWT token not included in requests.

issues.description: The lesson could benefit from mentioning Testcontainers for integration testing with the actual PostgreSQL database instead of H2
issues.webResearchNote: Testcontainers provides more realistic integration tests than H2 (which has different SQL dialect). This would be valuable for a capstone project.

issues.description: This lesson likely contains Railway deployment instructions similar to M14. The free tier discontinuation issue from M14.5 applies here as well
issues.webResearchNote: Must update deployment instructions to reflect Railway's discontinued free tier and suggest alternatives (Render, Fly.io, etc.).

issues.description: The deployment lesson should include a pre-deployment security checklist (environment variables, HTTPS, database credentials, JWT secrets)
issues.webResearchNote: A checklist would ensure students don't deploy with default passwords or exposed secrets. Items: change default passwords, set strong JWT secret, enable HTTPS, configure CORS for production domain.
